meaning: adj.(古希腊中南部)多里斯(Doris) 的,多里安人的n.多里安人
examples: He's a real Dorian Gray, apparently untouched by the ageing process.
The Dorian mode is one of the oldest diatonic scales.
"Maybe the bishop is the Dorian Gray of sleep," I suggested.
Ethnographers are entranced by a distinct dialect that continues to contain words from ancient Dorian Greek.
Dorian Gray emerged from the same dinner that insured the immortality of Sherlock Holmes.
